title: Exploiting the Intrinsic Neighborhood Structure for Source-free Domain Adaptation
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: This paper addresses source-free domain adaptation (SFDA), focusing on closed-set scenarios where source and target domains have the same categories. The authors observe that existing methods ignore the intrinsic neighborhood structure of target data in feature space, which can be valuable for SFDA. They propose a method that distinguishes between reciprocal and non-reciprocal neighbors, leveraging the finding that reciprocal neighbors are more trustworthy. The adaptation is achieved by pushing features towards reciprocal neighbors heavily.

methodology.key_insight: Target data of the same class forms clusters in the embedding space even after domain shift
methodology.approach: Exploiting the intrinsic neighborhood structure of target data
methodology.technique: Distinguishing between reciprocal and non-reciprocal neighbors
methodology.adaptation_method: Pushing features towards reciprocal neighbors heavily

results.observation: Approximately 75% of nearest neighbors have the correct label
results.finding: Reciprocal neighbors are more trustworthy than non-reciprocal nearest neighbors
results.visualization: t-SNE visualization shows significant cluster structure is preserved in target features

related_works: SHOT - for closed-set domain adaptation based on mutual information maximization and pseudo labeling
3C-GAN - for closed-set domain adaptation based on target-style image generation with a conditional GAN
BAIT - extends MCD to the SFDA setting
